title: Srrors'tok

{{Quote|Kneel … now|Ahnjai Rahmma|<ref name="G9" />}}
The '''Srrors'toks''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Hair/Legends|fur]]-covered, [[Tooth/Legends|fanged]], anthropomorphic [[Feline/Legends|felinoids]] native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Jankok]] in the [[Bozhnee sector/Legends|Bozhnee sector]]. They had a mostly non-technological society of [[Hunting/Legends|hunters]], characterized by their stern concept of honor and their [[Life debt/Legends|life debts]]. A Srrors'tok could be indentured to any being, which led some Srrors'toks to leave their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] to travel with an [[Alien/Legends|alien]] who had saved their lives. Such was the case with [[Tyionsis Cex]] and [[Ahnjai Rahmma]], two Srrors'toks who served the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Biology and appearance==
{{Quote|Then his lips parted, showing off those huge fangs of his—I guess that was his version of a smile. I saw his eyes when he did that—those cold, yellow cat-slit eyes. I don't mind telling you. It was enough to make my blood run cold.|[[Helkson Vall]], on [[Tyionsis Cex]]|<ref name="Name">{{WEGCite|book=Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters|story=What's In A Name}}</ref>}}
Srrors'toks were [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Feline/Legends|felinoids]],<ref name="E">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> although they had some [[Humanoid/Legends|anthropomorphic]] features. They were commonly 1.4 to 1.7 [[Meter/Legends|Imperial standard meters]] tall in adulthood, standing digitigrade on the four clawed toes of each of their hind [[Limb/Legends|limbs]]. They were usually burlier than a [[Human/Legends|Human]] of comparable height. Their long arms ended in three clawed fingers. A Srrors'tok walked as fast as a Human. Having evolved from [[Diet/Legends|predatorial]] creatures, a Srrors'tok had a [[Limb/Legends|tail]] and natural [[Tooth/Legends|fangs]] that could be used in combat to bite an opponent. The Srrors'tok face was relatively flat and featured two slit-pupiled [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], a distinct triangular [[Nose/Legends|nose]], prominent jowls from which grew whiskers, two large, triangular [[Ear/Legends|ears]], and a [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]] that featured a long, cleft upper lip into which slotted the U-shaped lower mandible.<ref name="AE" />

Srrors'toks easily endured high or medium temperatures, but they were uncomfortable when naked in cold climates because they were covered with short natural [[Hair/Legends|fur]] that did not keep them warm. Their pelage was commonly [[Color/Legends|golden]],<ref name="AE" /> yellow, or gray streaked with shades of brown.<ref name="G10" /> Certain Srrors'toks grew longer hair around their necks in the form of a mane; such accouterments did not share the color of the body fur.<ref name="G9" />

Some Srrors'toks developed the skill of ventriloquism, being able to generate grunts from different positions in a nearby area. The ability could be used to confuse prey, making them believe that they were surrounded by several Srrors'toks.<ref name="G10" />

==Society and culture==
{{Quote|As … you … (growl) … wish, Lord.|Ahnjai Rahmma to Antinnis Tremayne|<ref name="G9" />}}
The Srrors'toks lived in small groups that were linked by [[Blood/Legends|blood]] relations. The Srrors'toks in each tribe bonded with one another through certain activities, such as [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]] together. For the most part, Srrors'tok culture had not adapted to advanced technology by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], although some individuals used [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]]. Knowing that they were susceptible to cold weather, Srrors'toks commonly wore warm clothes when visiting inclement areas. Some [[Piercing/Legends|pierced]] their ears. Otherwise, Srrors'toks chose to avoid clothes and wore only bags to carry tools.<ref name="AE" />

The [[Language/Legends|language]] of this [[Species/Legends|species]], known as [[Hras'kkk'rarr]],<ref name="AE" /> used the sounds that they could produce naturally and easily<ref name="G9" />—namely growls, grunts, and clicks—combined in an intricate and elaborate way.<ref name="AE" /><ref name="G9" /> To fully communicate concepts, Hras'kkk'rarr also used non-verbal communication, including body poses and gestures.<ref name="AE" /><ref name="G9" /> Baring of fangs was thought by some to be the Srrors'tok equivalent of a smile.<ref name="Name" /> Communication with aliens was not easy for the Srrors'toks, as their [[Mouth/Legends|mouths]] had not evolved to pronounce [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] with ease. The Srrors'toks only attempted to say complex words in Basic if they knew the listener was already familiar with Basic spoken with the Srrors'tok dialect.<ref name="AE" />

===Honor concept===
[[File:Ahnjairahmma.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Ahnjai Rahmma, a Srrors'tok with a life-debt]]
An unsparing concept of honor was the basis of Srrors'tok society.<ref name="AE" /><ref name="G9" /> A member of the species could never betray any of his or her friends or any other Srrors'toks. Nor could a Srrors'tok abandon any of those people to their fate. Some Srrors'toks were unable to even conceive the abstract idea of doing so.<ref name="AE" />

An important part of this honor-bonded social structure was the Srrors'tok tradition of [[Life debt/Legends|life debts]], which was in many senses similar to that of the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]].<ref name="AE" /><ref name="G9" /> If a Srrors'tok's life was saved by another person, the Srrors'tok was obligated to pledge their life to serve the savior, sometimes as a [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]]. The life-debt only ended when either of the parties [[Death/Legends|died]]. If the Srrors'tok was unable to fulfill the life-debt—either by choice or, more commonly, by impossibility—the Srrors'tok was considered dishonored and was supposed to commit suicide as a punishment for the failure.<ref name="AE" /> Srrors'toks sometimes contracted a life-debt with a dishonorable person, an event that most Srrors'toks loathed; but, even in these cases, the Srrors'toks felt compelled to do their best to protect the individual.<ref name="G9" />

Sometimes, a Srrors'tok contracted two simultaneous life-debts, and in some cases the beneficiaries happened to be enemies of each other. In such a case, the Srrors'tok could decide to either consider the older life-debt to be canceled<ref name="AE" /> or to consider ''both'' life-debts to be canceled.<ref name="G9" /> However, Srrors'toks believed that contracting a second life-debt on purpose was a great shame,<ref name="AE" /><ref name="G9" /> speculating that, should such be allowed, their society would became a labyrinth of interwoven life-debts leading to the end of their culture as they knew it.<ref name="AE" />

==History==
The Srrors'toks evolved on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Jankok]],<ref name="AE" /> located in the [[Bozhnee sector/Legends|Bozhnee sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="Atlas">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> By the time of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Jankok had been discovered by the [[galactic community]]. However, as the [[Tree/Legends|rainforest]] world had few resources of interest, it was only rarely visited. Extraplanetary arrivals commonly included [[Scout/Legends|scouts]] and some [[Merchant/Legends|merchants]], who occasionally attempted to make deals with the native Srrors'toks. Due to that lack of interest, Jankok had no [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]].<ref name="AE" />

==Srrors'toks in the galaxy==
{{Quote|Now mind you, I'm not saying that Cex did it! My mother didn't raise no fool, no sir!.|Helkson Vall, on Tyionsis Cex|<ref name="Name" />}}
[[File:TyionsisCex-GG10.png|thumb|right|200px|Tyionsis Cex, a Srrors'tok bounty hunter]]
Very few Srrors'toks ever left Jankok—partially because they had little chance to do so due to the light traffic to their native planet.<ref name="AE" /> Nonetheless, Srrors'tok itinerant traders were not unheard of, and a number of Srrors'tok families settled on planets other than Jankok, such as [[Sellasas]].<ref name="G10" />

Certain Srrors'toks traveled through [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] as a consequence of having a life-debt with a highly mobile individual. The Srrors'tok [[Ahnjai Rahmma]] contracted a life-debt with [[High Inquisitor]] [[Antinnis Tremayne]] and became Tremayne's bodyguard,<ref name="G9" /><ref name="E" /> buffoon, and occasional [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]].<ref name="G9" /> A Srrors'tok with a life-debt might travel either in the company of that Srrors'tok savior or alone if the Srrors'tok could better serve the savior's interests by not being in the individual's presence. The Srrors'tok [[Tyionsis Cex]], born into a family of traders on Sellasas, lost his relatives during a raid of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] mostly directed against the local Imperial presence. Cex's life was then saved by Imperial [[Commander/Legends|commander]] [[Amara Jedselk]] and, due to his resulting life-debt to Jedselk, Cex eventually became an almost-independent [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]], accepting works for the Empire and seeing Jedselk only infrequently.<ref name="G10" /> Cex was once mocked by Imperial [[bureaucrat]] [[Matagorn]] as a lackey of Tremayne; shortly after Matagorn vanished with pieces of his body appearing in [[Gevis City]] and [[Verdson]].<ref name="Name" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Srrors'toks first appeared in the [[1993]] [[roleplaying game]] book ''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]'', by [[Simon Smith]] and [[Eric S. Trautmann]], in which the species was exemplified by the character of Ahnjai Rahmma. The illustration of a Srrors'tok that accompanied the game text was drawn by [[Mike Vilardi]]. [[1994|The following year]] a new Srrors'tok, Tyionsis Cex, was mentioned in ''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]''. The species was further developed in another roleplaying game book, ''[[Alien Encounters]]'', published in [[1998]].
